title: Home safe-state planning was corrected so progress no longer implied time remaining
expected_state: The Task-0022 planning baseline should have made the Home upload card treat `Safe on server` as a completed state with no progress bar, and any retained local copies should have appeared as separate muted text instead of looking like remaining transfer time.
actual_state: At the plan gate, the Home upload-card baseline still needed explicit correction because a visible progress treatment could still make safe or already-finished uploads read like `how long until done`, and retained local copies could still masquerade as upload progress until the human clarified the rule.
human_costs: the human had to restate the user-facing meaning of the Home card before planning could be approved
the planning baseline was not yet explicit enough about how safe state differs from active upload

human_intervention.summary: The human tightened the plan gate by stating that a Home progress bar is read as time remaining, so safe state must never show a bar and retained local copies must not look like transfer progress.

feedback_quotes: if Home shows a progress bar, humans will read it as 'how long until done'.
Safe state should never show a bar.
Retained local copies after upload should be separate muted text, not progress.

entries.target_state: The Home safe state should render as complete: no progress bar, no implied time remaining, and any retained local copies shown as separate muted text.
The Home card should not use a progress bar unless it truthfully reads as remaining time or active transfer progress to a human.
Progress treatment on Home should be reserved for sustained active upload, not for already-safe uploads or local-retention aftermath.
Elapsed progress should only appear where that progress actually helps the human understand an active upload state.
